jobs.description: We are looking for a skilled developer to create a Shopify app that enhances the shopping experience for our customers. The app should be user-friendly, well-integrated with Shopify's existing features, and must meet our specific functional requirements. If you have a proven track record in developing apps for Shopify and are familiar with Liquid, JavaScript, and API integrations, we would love to hear from you. Agency App This app is to make things easier for my clients. I have 600+ Stage #1 Chat feature that people can ask a question on how to do something basic on Shopify. The question pulls the answer from google gemini (Only text, no link to youtube video) Stage #2 The app gives me access to connect claude to there site so I can code on there theme. See this video for a great reference https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YatPsQlGM3g This feature needs to be locked for the app user unless we give them a code to unlock it Stage #3 Paid feature that if someone needs something done on there website they can pay for credits to talk to live support and they can fix the issue Tell me your favorite food. Send 5 examples of work you have done on Shopify. If you directly email me or add me on social media your application will also get declined straight away. I get over 100+ Emails a week of pitches to work with me. If you use AI to reply to me. This isn't personal. I'm looking for personal touch If you don't follow the above requests your application will be automatically declined without looking at it. Attention to detail is important to me. Cheers Clayton

jobs.description: I run a fast-growing ecommerce business and need someone to handle supplier communication, order tracking, and daily fulfillment updates. Your role is simple but critical: * Communicate with suppliers (China timezone) * Track orders and ensure they are shipped on time * Follow up on delays, issues, and missing tracking * Provide clear daily updates (no chasing needed) Requirements (read carefully): * Fast and responsive (no replying delays) * Comfortable working with China-based suppliers * Good written English (clear, concise) * Able to handle pressure and solve problems independently * Detail-oriented (no missed orders / wrong tracking) Daily Tasks: * Check order status and update tracking on all orders * Message suppliers and follow up until resolved * Flag issues early (delays, stock, shipping problems) * Send a daily report: * Orders shipped * Orders pending * Issues + action taken Price: $70/week to start (can increase based on performance) To apply, include: 1. A 30–60 second video explaining: * Your experience with suppliers / fulfillment * How you handle unresponsive suppliers 2. Write “OPERATOR” at the top of your proposal 3. Confirm your daily availability + timezone Applications without this will be ignored.
jobs.title: Ecommerce Operations VA (Supplier Communication + Order Tracking)

jobs.description: DTC Landing Page & Conversion Designer — Pet Health Brand (Figma) We're launching Ocupets, a premium canine vision support brand for the US market. Our complete copy is written across 9 documents and we need a designer to create high-converting page layouts in Figma. Pages to design (all desktop + mobile): • 1 Product Page (PDP) — this is the entire website. One single page that covers product, science, ingredients, 3-phase journey, testimonials, FAQ, pricing, guarantee, and brand story. CellSeed-style format with 11 product gallery image directions embedded in the copy doc. • 2 Pre-Landers — short bridge pages (1–2 scrolls). One for retargeting (stripped-down closer), one for cold traffic (symptom checker). • 2 Advertorials — long-form editorial landing pages. Must look like health articles, NOT sales pages. Single-column reading flow, no navigation distractions. • 2 Listicles — blog-style editorial pages. Clean, numbered sections. • 2 Branded PDFs — digital gift guides (8–12 pages and 15–20 pages). Must feel premium and worth the stated value. Total: 9 web page designs + 2 branded PDFs = 9 design deliverables. Premium, clean, health-focused. References: The Farmer's Dog, Seed, AG1, Hims, Grüns, CellSeed. The ideal candidate: • Has designed landing pages for DTC, health, or supplement brands • Works in Figma or Replo • Understands conversion design (visual hierarchy, CTA placement, mobile-first) • Can deliver within 7–10 working days Ideal Experience Shopify + Replo power user (must-have) Direct-to-consumer eCommerce funnels for paid social traffic (Meta, TikTok, YouTube) Strong copywriting instincts (hooks, offers, objections, FAQs) Ability to create/edit visuals (Figma, Canva, Photoshop, ChatGPT image tools, etc.) Familiar with A/B testing and interpreting analytics Requirements Proven experience in CRO for eCommerce product pages Ability to improve and generate high-quality product images without new photography Strong communication skills with the ability to deliver clear, actionable insights How to Apply (must include all 3): Share 1–5 Shopify/Replo pages you personally built (not just designed) Confirm how many hours per week you’re available Confirm you can write/edit copy (or use AI) and create images when needed Write or refine conversion-focused copy (or use AI) that resonates with our audience In addition, I need support with product imagery. I don’t want to hire a photographer or take new photos—instead, you’ll work with the images I provide, enhancing them or creating new variations using AI or editing tools to produce high-quality, conversion-focused visuals. Complete copy with image directions, brand identity files, and detailed brief ready to hand over immediately. Thanks, Dhruv, Ocupets
jobs.title: Figma/Replo Ecom Shopify Landing Page

jobs.description: MALT BRIEF — Server-Side Tracking Repair Meta CAPI · GA4 Measurement Protocol · Shopify Headless · Next.js / Vercel CONTEXT Zelder (zelder.fr) is a premium French e-commerce brand selling senior furniture. - Storefront: zelder.fr — Next.js on Vercel - Checkout: boutique.zelder.fr — native Shopify subdomain THE PROBLEM The Meta Pixel loses first-party cookie context when users transition from zelder.fr to boutique.zelder.fr at checkout. Purchase events are not tracked, ROAS attribution is severely underreported, and running reliable paid campaigns is currently impossible. ALREADY IN PLACE (PARTIAL) - Vercel API routes ready - Meta CAPI — partial implementation - GA4 Measurement Protocol — partial implementation - Shopify webhooks — partial TECH STACK - Frontend: Next.js (App Router) - Hosting: Vercel - E-commerce: Shopify Plus headless - Client tracking: Meta Pixel + GA4 gtag - Server tracking: Vercel API Routes (Node.js) - Webhooks: Shopify orders/create to API routes DELIVERABLES 1. Full tracking audit (pixel, CAPI, webhooks) 2. Meta CAPI via Vercel API Routes — ViewContent, AddToCart, InitiateCheckout, Purchase — with client/server deduplication via event_id 3. GA4 Measurement Protocol — same event coverage, clean cross-domain session stitching 4. Cross-domain parameter passing — fbclid, _fbc, _fbp and GA4 client_id must survive the storefront to checkout transition 5. Validation — Meta Event Manager match rate over 70%, GA4 DebugView clean, Purchase coverage over 95% + technical documentation SCREENING QUESTION "Have you worked on a setup where the storefront and checkout live on two separate domains? How did you handle first-party cookie persistence and attribution parameter passing between them?" If the answer doesn't mention cross-domain cookie loss or CAPI server-side relay, keep looking. MISSION FORMAT - Type: Fixed-scope - Duration: 3–5 days - Remote: Yes, 100% - Response: Within 48h
jobs.title: Tracking Repair Meta CAPI · GA4 Measurement Protocol · Shopify Headless · Next.js / Vercel

jobs.description: We are looking for an experienced e-commerce website developer to design and build a Shopify e-commerce website for a new premium children’s outerwear brand. This is a launch project. We are not just looking for someone to build a website, but someone who can guide us through the process, advise on best practices, and help create a strong, conversion-focused online store. We are launching with a limited product range (one core product in two colours), so the website must feel complete, premium, and engaging despite a small number of products. This is our first website, and we are looking for someone experienced who can work collaboratively with us to define the right structure, functionality, and user experience. *Scope of Work (to be defined collaboratively)* The selected developer will: - Lead initial scoping discussions to define website structure, page layout, required functionality, and best-practice UX/UI for conversion - Design and build a Shopify website (custom or theme-based) - Deliver a clean, modern, premium, mobile-first design - Develop key pages as a minimum including homepage, product page(s), about page, and FAQ page - Implement functionality as a minimum including product variants (size, colour), inventory and stock logic, email capture (e.g. back-in-stock, early access or similar), and a fast and simple checkout - Integrate systems as a minimum, including payments (Shopify Payments, Apple Pay, etc.), shipping and fulfilment systems, and analytics (e.g. Google Analytics, Meta Pixel) - Ensure the site is fast-loading and performance optimised *Collaboration Requirements* You will: - Work closely with our brand identity and content teams - Clearly define requirements for photography (product and lifestyle) and video content (demonstrating product functionality) - Advise on how to structure content to maximise engagement and conversion - Provide guidance suitable for a first-time e-commerce launch *Person Requirements* - Proven experience building Shopify e-commerce websites Must provide links to live websites you have developed (applications without this will not be considered) - Demonstrated experience with e-commerce / direct-to-consumer brands - Ability to clearly explain your approach and previous decisions - Ability to provide ongoing support following launch. *Application Requirements* If interested, please: - Provide links to live e-commerce websites you have developed and specify what you personally delivered on each project - Include a rough cost estimate for a project of this nature - Include a brief explanation of how you would approach this project Thank you and looking forward to your proposals.
jobs.title: E-commerce Web Developer for Premium Children’s Brand (Launch Project)

jobs.description: Hi, I am the founder of a fast-growing brand. I am NOT looking for a one time job and done but to help you grow and make money fi you do the same for me! THIS ROLE IS FOR EU TIMEZONE ONLY. If you are outside of Europe, please do not apply. I will not respond. You will own two things from day one: CUSTOMER SUPPORT — Handle all inbound tickets (email + DMs) — returns, sizing, complaints, order status — Handle all after sales processes ( Media, Email, Offline, phone ) — Hire other customer support team and train them — Reply in brand voice, not templates — Spot patterns in complaints before I do OPERATIONS & SUPPLY CHAIN — Monitor Shopify orders daily, flag anything unusual — Track and reconcile supplier invoices weekly — Update inventory and ops trackers in Google Sheets — Come to me with a problem summary AND a suggested fix, never just a forward YOU MUST HAVE: — EU timezone (non-negotiable) — Excellent written English & spoken English — Background in e-commerce, logistics, or operations — Shopify experience — Google Sheets comfort — Experience working independently without hand-holding TO APPLY YOU MUST RECORD A SHORT LOOM VIDEO 1. What relevant work have you done before? 2. One problem you spotted and fixed without being asked 3. Why you specifically would be great at this role NO LOOM VIDEO = NO REPLY. No exceptions. Exception: Only people who I Invite. This role to start with a clear path to full-time for the right person. I invest in people who perform. If you are sharp, proactive and take ownership, this role will grow with you. PS: The budget grows as we grow
jobs.title: E-Commerce Operations & Customer Support Coordinator (EU Timezone Only)

jobs.description: THE ROLE We run fast-growing fashion dropshipping stores across the EU and UK and need someone to fully own customer operations. This is not a ticket-answering job. You will manage complex cases, own the full supply chain communication, handle disputes and chargebacks, report directly to the founder, and lead our transition to AI-automated customer service. You will be a key hire with real ownership and a direct line to the founder. As we scale to multiple stores, your role grows with us. YOU MUST HAVE HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE IN - Credit card dispute and chargeback management. EU and UK consumer law context is a strong plus. - Refund and return management with a revenue-protection mindset - Supplier communication and supply chain follow-up in a dropshipping context - Shopify order management and shipping exception handling - Disputifier or similar dispute management tools - AI-powered CS workflows. Comfortable building and working inside AI-first operations - Reporting to a founder or senior stakeholder. Metrics, patterns, and weekly updates OUR CURRENT STACK Shopify | Disputifier | Custom AI tools | Credit card processors | PayPal (incoming) We are building toward 70% AI-automated customer service. You will help design and implement that system, not just work inside it. WHAT STRONG LOOKS LIKE - You speak in results. Not "I managed tickets" but "I reduced chargeback rate from X to Y" - You understand EU and UK consumer rights well enough to use them strategically, not just react to them - You have built or improved a process that outlasted you - You are comfortable being held accountable to numbers every week - You see a problem and fix it before you are asked WHAT YOU GET - Real ownership. You build the systems. You lead the operations. - Growth path. As we scale to multiple stores and markets, your role grows with us. - Fully remote. Work from anywhere. - Competitive salary based on experience. We do not hire based on CVs alone. We hire based on how people think and how they show up. If you have read this entire post and you recognize yourself in it, you already know what to do. if you read every word of this post and you genuinely believe you are the right person for this role, start your proposal with "i'm the one" in capital letters and in one sentence tell us why. The people who notice this are exactly the people we are looking for. TO APPLY, ANSWER THESE IN YOUR PROPOSAL 1. What e-commerce operations experience do you have? Be specific about tools, volume, and results. 2. A customer in the UK received the wrong item and is threatening a chargeback. Walk us through exactly what you do, step by step. 3. Describe one process you built or improved. What was broken, what did you change, what was the result? Proposals without answers to these three questions will not be reviewed.
jobs.title: Customer operations manager, Fashion e-commerce

jobs.description: A Life Plus | Contract-to-Hire | Immediate Start We’re scaling a DTC meal brand to $10M+ net profit. We don’t hire “growth managers.” We hire operators who test, kill, and scale fast. Your Mission Increase new customers per week while keeping CAC at or below cap. You will: -Launch fast probe tests (72-hour cycles) -Run single-variable experiments -Make clear kill / scale decisions -Use Shopify as source of truth (not platform CPA) Success = Output (Not Activity) -CAC decreases or stays under cap -New customers per week increases -Tests launched every week -Kill rules enforced consistently -Every test produces a learning What You’ll Actually Do -Audit live campaigns (Meta + Google) -Identify the #1 constraint on CAC -Launch 1–2 tests immediately -Test creatives (UGC, hooks, angles), audiences (broad, LAL, segments), and offers (if needed) -Kill losers fast, scale winners -Log: test → result → decision Not a Fit If You: -Need weeks to analyze before acting -Rely on ROAS instead of real CAC -Can’t define numeric kill rules -Prefer strategy decks over execution Requirements -Hands-on Meta (Facebook/Instagram) + Google Ads experience -Proven ability to reduce CAC or increase new customers in 7–14 days -Strong testing mindset (clear hypothesis → result → decision) -Comfortable working under strict CAC constraints -Can manage agencies inside a framework (not be managed by them) Setup -Hourly: $20–$50/hr -Start with a 7-day paid test sprint -Long-term role for top performers -Multiple operators hired — best performers stay
jobs.title: CAC Owner – Lower CAC Fast (Meta / Google | DTC eCommerce)

jobs.description: I run a Shopify pet brand (Big Zen Paws) and I’m currently running ads that are generating traffic and some initiate checkouts, but not many completed purchases. I’m looking for a Shopify expert who specializes in conversion rate optimization (CRO) to help identify what’s causing drop-off and improve my site’s performance. Scope of work: - Review and optimize product pages for higher conversion - Improve overall user experience (especially mobile) - Identify and fix friction in the checkout process - Suggest improvements for trust, layout, and flow My brand has a clean, elevated/luxury aesthetic, so it’s important that any changes maintain that look and feel. I’d prefer to start with a paid audit/consultation to understand what’s not working, and then potentially move forward with implementation. If you’ve worked with Shopify brands running paid ads and improving conversion rates, I’d love to hear about your experience and results

jobs.description: Remote (Spanish-speaking only) Summary We’re looking for a Graphic Designer specialized in ecommerce (Shopify), who is fast, execution-focused, and conversion-driven. We are not looking for someone who only creates nice-looking designs — we need someone who understands how to build product pages and creatives that sell. You will work in a dynamic environment supported by AI tools, creating product pages, ad creatives, and visual assets to scale marketing campaigns. Key Responsibilities Design and build Shopify product pages using the theme editor (no coding required) Create high-converting visual creatives for ads and social media Design product images (mockups, before/after, benefits, etc.) using Canva and AI tools Adapt page structures based on competitor research Maintain consistent brand identity across all assets Work efficiently across multiple tasks and projects Requirements Proven experience in ecommerce (Shopify, dropshipping, or similar) Strong Canva skills (required) + basic knowledge of Adobe tools Experience creating product pages and creatives that convert Strong visual judgment focused on performance (not just aesthetics) Ability to follow instructions and replicate structures Fast execution and good organization Plus: Basic video editing skills Experience with AI tools (Midjourney, etc.) What we are NOT looking for Designers focused only on corporate branding Slow or overly perfectionist profiles Candidates without ecommerce experience Benefits Competitive compensation Fully remote work Fast-paced, results-driven environment Real opportunity for growth within the team Deliverables 1. Shopify Product Pages Build complete landing pages using existing templates Adapt structure based on competitor analysis Support content creation (AI + internal copywriter) 2. Ad Creatives & Social Media Assets Create images for ads (Facebook, Instagram, etc.) Optimize visuals for conversion and engagement Adapt formats for different platforms 3. Email & Marketing Materials Email headers Promotional graphics Campaign visuals 4. Product Mockups & Packaging Create product visuals Basic packaging mockups aligned with brand 5. Asset Organization Maintain organized files in Google Drive / Canva Deliver assets ready for immediate use Workload 2–3 product pages per week 8–12 creatives per week (ads/social media) Important This is an execution-focused role We value speed, consistency, and performance Execution matters more than perfect design How to Apply Please include: Examples of product pages you’ve created Examples of ad creatives or social content Tools you usually work with Applications without examples will not be considered. Start your application with the word “Shopify” to confirm you have read the full job description.

jobs.description: Freelancers only. No agencies. IMPORTANT: Loom Video Required (No Written Proposals) Submit ONLY a Loom video link. No text, no portfolio links, no additional information. Applications with anything other than a Loom link will be ignored. In your Loom video, walk through: • Your Shopify backend (code / Liquid structure) • Theme customizer usage • A live store you’ve built • How you approach pixel-perfect implementation About Us We’re a Shopify-focused eCommerce agency building high-converting stores for DTC brands. Our workflow is fast, structured, and built around AI-assisted development (Claude Code). We prioritize precision, speed, and clean execution. What You’ll Own • Shopify theme customization (Dawn and similar frameworks) • Building pages and sections from Figma with strict pixel-perfect accuracy • PDP, homepage, and landing page development • App integrations and custom functionality • Performance optimization, bug fixes, and ongoing iteration All development runs through our AI-assisted Claude Code workflow. This is a core part of how we operate. What We’re Looking For • Strong Shopify fundamentals (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript) • Ability to translate Figma designs into exact builds, not approximate • High attention to detail (spacing, typography, responsiveness) • Comfortable with AI-assisted development (Claude Code or similar) • Clean, scalable, maintainable code (no messy inline styles) • Fast turnaround and clear communication • Ability to work independently and take ownership • Some EST overlap preferred What You’ll Be Building We build complex, interactive Shopify sections. Examples: • mood.com Shop by Mood toggle: changes background, imagery, and active state dynamically • gruns.co/#offers Subscription buy box: toggle pricing, update UI states, handle variants • nectarsleep.com Homepage hero: structured layout with trust signals and strong CTA hierarchy If you can take a live section and recreate it pixel-perfectly in Dawn, including all interactions, this role will be a strong fit. Working Style • Figma files provided for every build • Daily async updates via ClickUp • Async-first, but responsiveness within a few hours during EST hours is expected Schedule • Full-time (8 hours/day, Monday to Friday) • Daily progress updates required • Ability to iterate quickly during active projects Compensation • $1,000 to $1,500/month depending on experience • Performance-based growth • Long-term opportunity Hiring Process • Shortlisted candidates may receive a small trial task

jobs.description: Hi, I’m looking for a Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) specialist to review my WooCommerce store and identify opportunities to improve conversions and overall customer experience. My store is a kawaii-themed ecommerce shop focused on “mystery bundle” products (scoops + premium gachas). Most of my traffic comes from TikTok, so the audience is highly visual and impulse-driven. What I’m looking for: - A full CRO audit of my website (especially product pages and checkout) - Identify friction points affecting conversions - Suggestions to improve product page clarity, trust, and buying motivation - Recommendations to improve bundle presentation and increase AOV - Feedback on mobile vs desktop experience - Actionable, specific suggestions (not generic advice) Important: - The site currently has some technical fixes in progress (cart, layout, etc.), so this is focused on conversion and UX improvements after those are resolved - Experience with ecommerce (preferably WooCommerce) is required - Experience with bundles, upsells, or similar products is a big plus Deliverable: - A clear list of prioritized recommendations (what to fix first, what will have biggest impact) - Optional: quick mockups or examples if relevant This is a small initial project, but if we’re a good fit, there may be ongoing work. To apply, please include: - Examples of ecommerce stores you’ve optimized - Results you’ve achieved (conversion rate, AOV, etc.) - Your approach to CRO audits Thank you!
jobs.title: Ecommerce CRO Specialist for WooCommerce Store (Conversion Optimization, UX & AOV Improvement)

jobs.description: We need a detail-oriented QA specialist to test a Shopify supplement store after theme/CRO work and checkout setup. This is a hands-on validation role. We want someone who can systematically check the customer journey, catch broken details, and verify that tracking is firing correctly. Main scope: _test product pages, collections, cart drawer, checkout flow, and post-purchase flow _verify links, buttons, menus, banners, forms, popups, and navigation _check mobile and desktop behavior _validate subscription flow and bundle behavior _test Checkout Champ flow and transitions from storefront to checkout _verify that key tracking tags/events are firing correctly _flag UX issues, broken logic, visual issues, and edge-case errors Tracking / validation scope includes: _GTM / existing tracking tag checks _event validation for key ecommerce actions _basic pixel / tag sanity checks _confirmation that no obvious duplicate or missing events exist _checkout / thank-you flow validation Important: _we do not want vague “looks fine” QA _we want structured testing and precise bug reporting _experience with Shopify ecommerce QA is required _tracking QA familiarity is a strong plus Please send: _2–3 relevant QA examples for Shopify or ecommerce _the kinds of bugs/issues you usually catch _your process for testing links, forms, checkout, and tracking _whether you have experience validating GTM / pixels / ecommerce events
jobs.title: Shopify QA Specialist for Storefront, Checkout, Links, and Tracking Validation

jobs.title: Shopify Expert for Online Store Growth

jobs.description: We’re hiring a Shopify developer to help upgrade a DTC supplement store into a more premium, conversion-focused storefront. This is not a full custom build from scratch. We want someone strong in Shopify theme implementation, product page UX, cart optimization, and clean execution. Main scope: _implement and customize a premium Shopify theme (Impact preferred) _build high-converting product page templates _improve cart drawer / cart UX _create lightweight, editable Shopify sections and blocks _support a more premium, credible supplement brand presentation _install and configure Checkout Champ _connect Checkout Champ cleanly with our Shopify setup and payment flow _keep the build clean, fast, and mobile-first Expected product page work includes: _review stars / social proof placement _benefit bullet sections _trust badges _sticky add-to-cart _quantity / pack offers _delivery ETA block _FAQ / ingredients / usage / COA sections _complementary product placement _better subscription presentation Cart / merchandising work includes: _stronger cart drawer structure _free shipping progress bar _cross-sell / upsell placement _clearer checkout CTA _cleaner mobile experience Checkout / funnel scope includes: _Checkout Champ installation and initial configuration _branded checkout/funnel setup _alignment between storefront flow and checkout flow _clean handoff for future optimization/testing Existing / planned stack: _Shopify _Impact theme _Judge.me _Shopify Search & Discovery _Appstle Subscriptions _Shopify Bundles _existing Stape / server-side GTM tracking _Checkout Champ _Authorize.net You should be comfortable building within this stack and only suggest alternatives when there is a clear UX, performance, or maintainability reason. Important: _no bloated app-heavy approach _no messy hardcoded changes _custom sections must be lightweight and easy to edit in Shopify admin _product-specific content should use metafields / dynamic sources where relevant _strong mobile execution matters Please send: _2–3 relevant Shopify stores _what you personally built on each one _any Checkout Champ or similar checkout/funnel setup experience _your approach for PDP + cart + editable custom sections
jobs.title: Shopify Theme / CRO Developer for Premium Supplement Store

jobs.description: We are looking for a skilled and efficient web developer to design and develop a modern, responsive 5-page website for our shopping store within a tight deadline of 3 days. The website should be visually appealing, user-friendly, and optimized for performance. *Project Scope:* * Develop a 5-page website: 1. Home Page 2. Shop / Products Page 3. Product Details Page 4. About Us Page 5. Contact Us Page * Responsive design (mobile, tablet, desktop) * Basic e-commerce functionality (product listing, add to cart, etc.) * Clean and modern UI/UX design * Fast loading speed and SEO-friendly structure * Contact form integration * Social media integration *Requirements:* * Proven experience in building e-commerce or shopping websites * Strong skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript (or frameworks like React, or CMS like WordPress/Shopify) * Ability to work under tight deadlines and deliver within 3 days * Experience with payment gateway integration is a plus *Deliverables:* * Fully functional 5-page website * Source code / CMS access * Basic documentation for managing the website *Timeline:* * *Strict deadline: 3 days* *Budget:* * Please submit your proposal with your estimated cost based on the timeline *How to Apply:* * Share your portfolio or previous similar projects * Briefly explain how you will complete this project within 3 days * Mention your preferred technology stack We are looking for someone reliable who can deliver high-quality work quickly.
jobs.title: Build a 5-Page E-commerce Website for Shopping Store

jobs.description: We are migrating from Omnisend to Klaviyo for a Shopify DTC supplement brand. We need a specialist who can handle both: _clean migration of audience and consent data _optimization of lifecycle flows, automations, and onsite forms Scope: _migrate audience from Omnisend to Klaviyo without losing key list quality _preserve and map important profile properties, tags, and consent data _recreate or improve core segments inside Klaviyo _rebuild and optimize essential flows (welcome, abandoned cart, browse abandonment, post-purchase, winback) _set up and optimize popup / flyout / embedded forms _ensure forms, lists, and flows are connected correctly _prevent duplicate sending or broken automation during migration _support cutover from Omnisend to Klaviyo cleanly Important: _this is not just a data import job _we want someone strong in migration + lifecycle strategy + Klaviyo execution _Shopify ecommerce experience is required _supplement / wellness / DTC experience is a plus Please send: _2–3 relevant Klaviyo migration examples _what platforms you migrated from/to _which flows and forms you personally built _your migration approach to avoid list damage or automation issues
jobs.title: Klaviyo Migration & Lifecycle Specialist for Shopify DTC Brand

jobs.description: We have a well-established Shopify store that currently has a low PageSpeed Insights score. We are looking for an expert developer experienced in improving Shopify page loading speed to audit, give advice and implement solutions for improving our page load speed. The aim of increasing the page load speed is to help our store rank better (SEO), easier AI crawling and improve overall user experience.
jobs.title: Shopify Store Page Load Speed Increase

If you have a strong background in Shopify development and a passion for creative content, we’d love to hear from you!
jobs.title: Shopify Blog Post Development using RecipeKit

jobs.description: I need someone to have a look at our website, for over a week the sessions have dropped off reading correct and we have no ATC showing on the shopify or google GA4 . Something has gone wrong so you will need to problem solve and figure out the issue

jobs.description: We have developed a free Shopify app designed to improve store performance and user experience. Key features include: • Wishlist functionality • Trust badges • SEO optimization tools • Page speed optimization We are looking for experienced Shopify developers who actively manage client stores and are open to testing new tools. Your role will be to: • Install the app on suitable client stores (with full client permission) • Configure and test the features based on store needs • Monitor performance and usability over 1–2 weeks • Share structured feedback on functionality, UX, and improvements If your client finds the app valuable, you may encourage them to share their honest, voluntary feedback on the Shopify App Store. (No incentives or pressure involved.) Requirements • Currently managing at least 3 active Shopify client stores • Must have client approval before installing any app • Proven Shopify development experience (visible on profile) • Strong communication and client handling skills To Apply, Please Answer: How many active Shopify client stores do you currently manage? Please share 1–2 store URLs you’ve worked on recently. How open are your clients to testing new apps or tools?
jobs.title: Shopify Developer Needed to Test Free App on Client Stores & Provide Feedback

jobs.description: 💼 Who We're Looking For We’re looking for a Senior Fashion eCommerce Strategist & Planner who understands exactly what to launch, when to launch it, and why it will convert — per market. This is a pure strategy role. No basic execution. No hand-holding. You are the person who: Knows what’s trending before it peaks Understands seasonal timing per country Can map out campaign calendars that drive revenue If you can confidently say: 👉 “This is what we should launch next week in France — and this is why” …then keep reading. 🧠 About Us We’re a fast-growing Dutch DTC fashion group with 6 active stores across Europe, with our core markets: 🇫🇷 France 🇳🇱 Netherlands 🇬🇧 United Kingdom We are building scalable fashion brands, not just running stores. Our model: Weekly campaigns (themes, drops, flash sales) Performance-driven mindset (Meta + Google Shopping) Fast iteration based on data We need someone who can bring structure, direction, and winning ideas into this machine. 🎯 What You'll Work On As our Strategist & Planner, you will own the campaign calendar and market direction. Your responsibility: Define what goes live, when, and in which market Build weekly & monthly campaign planning Identify themes, hooks, and angles per country (FR / NL / UK) Advise on which products & collections to push Spot opportunities for: Flash sales Seasonal campaigns Cultural moments Trend-based drops Translate trends into clear action plans for the team Work closely with design & media buyers to align execution You will NOT just suggest ideas — You will create structured plans we can execute immediately. 🧩 You Should Be Able To... Understand fashion trends across EU markets (FR / NL / UK) Predict what products & styles will perform next Build campaign calendars (weekly + monthly) Translate trends into themes, offers, and angles Think in revenue, not just aesthetics Align strategy with paid media & Google Shopping Identify market-specific behavior differences Move fast and continuously optimize based on results 🇫🇷 🇳🇱 🇬🇧 Market Understanding Is Key You must understand differences like: France → more aesthetic, emotional, brand-driven Netherlands → more direct, value-driven, offer-focused UK → mix of trend + price sensitivity + fast-moving campaigns And know how to adapt: Themes Messaging Timing Product focus ✅ What We Expect Proven experience in fashion eCommerce strategy (non-negotiable) Strong track record with campaign planning & trend forecasting Experience across multiple EU markets (FR/NL/UK preferred) Ability to independently generate high-quality ideas weekly Structured thinker — you bring clarity, not chaos Fluent English Fast communication & proactive mindset Long-term vision — we are building brands We are looking for a true A-player strategist — not someone who just follows trends, but someone who translates them into profit. 📝 To Apply, Answer These: Give an example of a fashion campaign calendar you created. How do you decide what products or themes to push in a given week? What differences do you see between FR, NL, and UK markets? What would you launch next week in: France Netherlands UK (Be specific: theme + product + angle) How do you stay ahead of fashion trends and seasonal shifts? Have you worked with performance marketing teams (Meta / Google)? How do you align with them? Why are you the right person to guide our campaign direction across multiple brands? ⏰ This is NOT for beginners. If you are a “Pinterest trend saver” — skip this. If you need direction instead of giving it — this is not your role. But if you: Think ahead See opportunities others miss And can turn trends into structured, revenue-driving plans Then we want to hear from you.
jobs.title: Fashion eCommerce Strategist (Campaign Planning & Market Strategy | EU Focus)

jobs.description: We are a fast-growing e-commerce company based in Sweden, operating globally with a strong focus on DTC and high-volume order fulfillment. We are looking for a skilled and proactive Shopify E-Commerce Manager to take ownership of our store and help us scale to the next level. This is not just a maintenance role — we want someone who can actively grow the business, improve conversion rates, and help launch new ideas and product pages. 🔧 Key Responsibilities: Manage and optimize our Shopify store (products, collections, apps, UX) Oversee inventory and product listings (we handle 100s of SKUs) Improve conversion rate (CRO) across product pages and cart Implement and execute SEO strategies (on-page, technical, content) Analyze performance data (Shopify, GA, ads) and identify growth opportunities Build and launch new landing pages, bundles, and offers Test new ideas (funnels, upsells, pricing strategies, etc.) Ensure smooth day-to-day operations of the store 🚀 What We’re Looking For: Strong experience with Shopify (themes, apps, backend) Proven track record in growing e-commerce stores Understanding of CRO, UX, and customer journey Data-driven mindset (comfortable with analytics & reporting) Ability to take initiative and execute ideas independently Experience with international stores is a plus 💡 Bonus Skills (Not Required but Valuable): Experience with subscriptions, bundles, or upsells Familiarity with Klaviyo or email marketing flows Basic design skills (Canva/Figma) Experience launching new product lines or stores 🌍 About Us: We are a rapidly scaling e-commerce brand with global customers and high order volume. We move fast, test constantly, and are always looking for new ways to grow. Important: When applying, please include: Examples of Shopify stores you have worked on What you would improve first on a store like ours A short explanation of your biggest e-commerce win
jobs.title: Shopify E-Commerce Manager (Growth-Focused, Fast Scaling Brand)

jobs.description: We are looking for an experienced bookkeeping professional to assist with Shopify and Amazon payout reconciliation within Xero. In this role, you will be responsible for reconciling payouts received from Shopify and Amazon and ensuring they are accurately recorded in Xero. This includes matching sales, fees, refunds, and deposits to bank transactions and ensuring all records are properly categorized and aligned. You will also be expected to identify and resolve discrepancies between platform reports and bank statements, ensuring that revenue, fees, and net payouts are correctly reflected in the financial records. The goal is to maintain accurate and clean bookkeeping for all ecommerce sales channels. We are looking for someone with strong experience in Xero and ecommerce bookkeeping, particularly with Shopify and Amazon reconciliation. Familiarity with payout structures, fees, and integrations is highly important. The ideal candidate should be detail-oriented, reliable, and able to work independently while maintaining accuracy in complex transaction environments.
jobs.title: Shopify/Amazon Payout Reconciliation

jobs.description: Job Description: I need someone to help me set up my Shopify store and teach me how to maintain it. I have ADHD, so I need someone who communicates clearly, is patient, and can break tasks into simple steps. Tasks: Set up my Shopify store Add products, collections, and images Install apps Fix layout/navigation Record simple Loom videos showing me how to do things Explain important things that I would need to know clearly Requirements: Shopify experience (please send examples) Clear communication Patient and organized Good English Budget: $4–$11 per hour depending on experience. Will hope to find someone I can work with ongoing as the site will be added to weekly & will need ongoing advice. To Apply: Please answer: Your hourly rate Shopify stores you’ve worked on Your working hours A short example of how you explain things simply
jobs.title: Shopify Virtual Assistant Needed (Setup + Teach Me How to Use It/Maintain It)

jobs.title: Shopify Website for Aquarium Business

jobs.description: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager (Growth & Revenue) - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand We’re looking for someone who knows how to turn traffic into profit. Not someone who just tweaks pages. Not someone who runs random A/B tests. We want a growth operator who understands: 👉 What makes people buy 👉 How to increase profit per visitor 👉 How to build offers, funnels, and systems that drive real revenue 🧠 About Us We’re a fast-growing DTC eCommerce brand doing ~$1M/month, primarily through Meta (Facebook) ads, targeting US/UK/CA/AU markets. Our product sits in a highly emotional niche (health / relief / transformation), where messaging, positioning, and belief systems drive conversions. You’ll be working directly with the founder and a creative strategist in a fast-moving, performance-driven environment. 🎯 Your Core Mission 👉 Increase profit per session, not just conversion rate This includes: Improving conversion rate Increasing AOV Expanding LTV Building stronger offers 🔥 What You’ll Be Responsible For 1. Offer & Monetization Optimization Build and test new offers, bundles, and pricing structures Increase AOV and upsell take rate Identify and implement cross-sells and post-purchase offers Suggest new products based on data and customer behavior 2. Landing Pages & Funnels Build and iterate on high-converting landing pages Translate winning ad angles into page structure, copy, and flow Create: New main landing page Angle-specific landing pages Advertorial funnels 3. CRO Testing Engine Run continuous A/B tests (minimum 2 live at all times) Use tools like Intelligems (and potentially Triple Whale) Identify: Bottlenecks Drop-off points Revenue leaks 4. Data → Insights → Revenue Turn data into clear, actionable insights Focus on: Profit per session Conversion rate AOV / upsell performance Work closely with: Creative strategist (messaging & angles) Founder (strategy & direction) 👉 You will connect ad performance with on-site monetization 5. Customer Journey Optimization Improve key pages: Product page Homepage About page Optimize the entire buying experience from click to purchase 6. AI-Driven Execution Use AI tools for: Copywriting Research Creative ideation Move fast from idea → test → result 🧩 Tools & Stack Shopify (custom Shrine theme) Replo Figma Intelligems Potentially Triple Whale 📈 90-Day Success Metrics Increase CVR from ~3% → 4%+ Increase profit per session Improve AOV and upsell take rate Launch: 1 new winning landing page 2 Iterations on top-performing landing page so they are angle-specific towards the top performing angles Maintain 2+ active tests at all times ✅ What We’re Looking For 3+ years in eCommerce (Shopify, DTC) Experience in English-speaking markets (US, UK, CA, AU) Strong understanding of: Direct response copywriting Landing page psychology Offer creation & pricing AOV / LTV optimization Ability to: Execute (not just strategize) Think in revenue, not just conversion rate Work fast and independently 🚫 This Is NOT For You If You only analyze data but don’t execute You rely on developers for every small change You’ve only worked on small or low-traffic stores You don’t understand offers, upsells, or monetization You don’t use AI to increase speed and output You don't know how to manage your time efficiently and focus only on the tasks that have the biggest ROI potential. 💰 Compensation $4,000–$5,000/month Part-time (20 hours/week) Performance-based bonus tied to: Profit per session Revenue growth Conversion improvements 🔥 Why This Role Is Different You’ll work on a high-volume store with real data You’ll have direct impact on revenue You’ll help shape offers, not just pages You’ll operate in a fast, no-BS environment focused on results 📩 How to Apply Include: 2–3 examples of stores or funnels you’ve worked on Specific results (CVR, AOV, revenue impact) An example of how you increased AOV or LTV Your approach to improving profit per session and LTV If you’ve read this far, include the word “PROFIT” in your application so we know you pay attention to detail.
jobs.title: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand

jobs.description: Texas only Please!!!!! Hi! I run a brand called Fishers of Men Outdoors — we sell Christian fishing apparel (shirts, hats, tumblers, etc.). I’m looking for someone to help me set up and scale our full ecommerce system. Specifically, I need: • TikTok Shop created + ads setup and management • Facebook & Instagram Shop created + ads setup and management • Shopify store optimization (clean up layout, improve product pages, organize into 3 product lines) • Proper domain setup and optimization (FishersofMenOutdoors.net) • Help getting everything connected and running smoothly for sales I’m looking to start immediately and build something long-term if it works out. Can you let me know: 1. Have you done full setups like this before? 2. Examples of Shopify/TikTok Shop work you’ve done 3. How quickly you can get started Looking forward to hearing from you — thank you!
jobs.title: Fishers of Men Outdoors
